name: Claude Opus 4.8
verdict: Instantly readable: a proper walrus with both tusks and whiskers, an upright cello with a scroll, strings and an endpin. The f-holes went missing, and the bow — suspiciously walrus-colored — seems to have fused with the flipper holding it. Roughly 5 of 7 on the checklist.

name: GPT-5.5
verdict: The most polished drawing of the batch — gradients, shading, tiny f-holes, a confident endpin. But the composition turns surreal: the cello is fused into the walrus's belly, the scroll floats above its head like a hair curl, the bow slices right through the tusks — and the walrus has no legs at all, the body just fades out below. Beautiful chaos; roughly 5 of 7.

name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
verdict: The artist of the group: a dramatic spotlight, closed eyes, magnificent whiskers — this walrus clearly feels the music. The cello has strings, a bridge, an endpin and a bow placed right at the bridge, but only one f-hole made it, and the scroll ended up as a little curl on the walrus's head — a strangely popular spot. Roughly 6 of 7.

name: Grok 4.5
verdict: Charming children's-book style, and the checklist is nearly all here: both tusks, whiskers, two proper f-holes, four strings, a tailpiece and an endpin. The cello, however, is shaped like a barrel with hoops, and the bow rests on a flipper-blob rather than being held. Roughly 5 of 7.
